title: 🔬 DORA × Copilot [Edu] [v2]
description: Cross-analysis of DORA engineering metrics segmented by Copilot adoption cohort, revealing the impact of AI assistance on software delivery performance.

list.name: environment
list.label: Environment
list.definition: SELECT DISTINCT environment FROM deployments ORDER BY environment
list.query: SELECT DISTINCT environment FROM deployments ORDER BY environment

panels.description: Data mode banner — shows whether this dashboard displays live GitHub data (green), synthetic seed data (amber), or demo data (blue).

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  CASE mode
    WHEN 'live' THEN '📡 Live Data — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    WHEN 'seed' THEN '🌱 Synthetic Seed Data — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    WHEN 'demo' THEN '🎮 Demo Environment — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    ELSE '❓ Unknown Mode'
  END AS "Data Source"
FROM data_mode
ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT 1

options.content:
# 🔬 DORA × Copilot Cohort

**Supplementary Metrics — Copilot impact on DORA performance**

## What it Shows
This dashboard compares the four DORA metrics side-by-side for two cohorts: developers with an active Copilot seat versus those without. It surfaces whether Copilot usage correlates with faster lead times, more frequent deployments, or lower failure rates.

## Why it Matters
Cohort comparisons provide the strongest available signal (short of a controlled experiment) for Copilot's impact on engineering velocity and quality. If Copilot-active developers consistently show better DORA scores, it strengthens the case for broader adoption and investment.

> ⚠️ **Attribution caveat**: Cohort membership is determined by active seat status (last activity within 28 days) — not by confirmed Copilot use on specific PRs. Selection bias may exist if higher-performing developers are also more likely to adopt new tools.

## How to Interpret
- **Lead Time / Cycle Time**: Lower values for Copilot cohort suggest faster code delivery
- **PR throughput**: Higher weekly PR counts for Copilot cohort indicate productivity gains
- **Lines changed**: More lines per developer in Copilot cohort may reflect reduced friction writing code

## How to Use This Data
- Use this dashboard to build the business case for Copilot investment
- Combine with the **Copilot Adoption** dashboard to ensure the active cohort is large enough to be representative
- Track trends over time — short-term spikes may reflect novelty effects rather than sustained productivity gains

panels.description: DORA × Copilot Cohort — compares DORA metrics between Copilot-active and non-active developers.

panels.title: Change Lead Time: Copilot vs Non-Copilot

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT
  CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS "Cohort",
  ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
    ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (d.created_at - pr.merged_at)) / 3600.0
  )::numeric, 1) AS "Lead Time (hours)"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_pull_requests dpr ON dpr.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.number = dpr.pr_number
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE d.environment = '$environment'
  AND ds.state = 'success'
  AND pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1

panels.description: copilot_users CTE uses fixed NOW()-28d. Bridge resolver required. Observational only — correlation ≠ causation.

panels.title: PR Cycle Time: Copilot vs Non-Copilot

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT
  CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS "Cohort",
  ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
    ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (pr.merged_at - pr.created_at)) / 3600.0
  )::numeric, 1) AS "PR Cycle Time (hours)"
FROM pull_requests pr
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1

panels.description: ⚠️ Proxy cohort: PR author's current seat status. Calendar time. N not shown — verify sample size before drawing conclusions.

options.content:
## 📖 Change Lead Time: Copilot vs Non-Copilot — Learning Guide

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/pulls/pulls)
`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls` + bridge resolver + `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`pull_requests`, `deployment_pull_requests`, `deployments`, `copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Bar chart comparing median lead time for 'Copilot Active' vs 'Non-Copilot' PR authors. With copilot_users CTE.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT
  CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS "Cohort",
  ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
    ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (d.created_at - pr.merged_at)) / 3600.0
  )::numeric, 1) AS "Lead Time (hours)"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_pull_requests dpr ON dpr.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.number = dpr.pr_number
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE d.environment = '$environment'
  AND ds.state = 'success'
  AND pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **copilot_users CTE** — SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'. Fixed 28-day window, not Grafana range.
- **Bridge resolver required** — only PRs linked to deployments included; unlinked PRs excluded from both cohorts.
- **Selection bias** — senior developers often get Copilot seats first; faster lead times may reflect experience.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Statistical significance of the lead time difference.
- Causality — observational comparison only.

panels.title: 📖 Change Lead Time: Copilot vs Non-Copilot — Learning Guide

options.content:
## 📖 PR Cycle Time: Copilot vs Non-Copilot — Learning Guide

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/pulls/pulls)
`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls` + `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`pull_requests`, `copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Bar chart comparing median PR cycle time (merged_at - created_at) for Copilot vs non-Copilot PR authors.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT
  CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS "Cohort",
  ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
    ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (pr.merged_at - pr.created_at)) / 3600.0
  )::numeric, 1) AS "PR Cycle Time (hours)"
FROM pull_requests pr
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **Calendar time** — wall-clock including nights, weekends, draft periods.
- **Cohort balance not shown** — chart doesn't display N per bar; unequal cohort sizes make comparison unreliable.
- **Seat roster timing** — current seat list; developer who had a seat during time range but lost it since = Non-Copilot.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Statistical significance of the cycle time difference.
- Effect size — is a 2-hour difference meaningful?

panels.title: 📖 PR Cycle Time: Copilot vs Non-Copilot — Learning Guide
Merged PRs by Cohort (weekly)

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', pr.merged_at) AS time,
  CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS "Cohort",
  COUNT(*) AS "PRs"
FROM pull_requests pr
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY 1

panels.description: Current seat roster attributes all past PRs retroactively. No cohort-size normalization — compare rates, not raw counts.

panels.title: Lines Changed by Cohort (weekly)

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', pr.merged_at) AS time,
  CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS "Cohort",
  SUM(COALESCE(pr.additions, 0) + COALESCE(pr.deletions, 0)) AS "Lines Changed"
FROM pull_requests pr
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY 1

panels.description: Lines = pr.additions + pr.deletions (renames double-count). Auto-generated files inflate. Current seat roster.

options.content:
## 📖 Merged PRs by Cohort (weekly) — Learning Guide

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/pulls/pulls)
`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls?state=closed` + `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`pull_requests`, `copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Weekly time series of merged PR counts split by Copilot/non-Copilot PR author cohort.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', pr.merged_at) AS time,
  CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS "Cohort",
  COUNT(*) AS "PRs"
FROM pull_requests pr
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY 1
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **Roster timing mismatch** — today's seat list attributes ALL past PRs retroactively.
- **No per-developer normalization** — if Copilot cohort has 10 people and non-Copilot has 50, raw counts are misleading.
- **Mutually exclusive split** — hard binary; developers never in both cohorts simultaneously.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Per-developer PR rate (normalize by cohort headcount).
- PR complexity or size differences between cohorts.

panels.title: 📖 Merged PRs by Cohort (weekly) — Learning Guide

options.content:
## 📖 Lines Changed by Cohort (weekly) — Learning Guide

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/pulls/pulls)
`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls` + `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`pull_requests`, `copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Weekly sum of (pr.additions + pr.deletions) for each cohort.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', pr.merged_at) AS time,
  CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS "Cohort",
  SUM(COALESCE(pr.additions, 0) + COALESCE(pr.deletions, 0)) AS "Lines Changed"
FROM pull_requests pr
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY 1
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **additions + deletions double-counts** — a 10-line rename = 20 lines changed.
- **Auto-generated files** — lockfile regenerations or generated clients have high line counts with minimal developer effort.
- **Same roster timing caveat** — current seat list, not historical.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Code complexity or meaningful change volume.
- Whether large line counts represent valuable engineering work.

panels.title: 📖 Lines Changed by Cohort (weekly) — Learning Guide
Change Fail Rate: Copilot vs Non-Copilot

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
),
deploy_cohorts AS (
  SELECT
    d.deployment_id,
    CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS cohort
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_pull_requests dpr ON dpr.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.number = dpr.pr_number
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
  WHERE ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.environment = '$environment'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
),
failures AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT d.deployment_id
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  JOIN issues i ON i.labels::jsonb @> '[{"name":"incident"}]'
    AND i.created_at BETWEEN d.created_at AND d.created_at + INTERVAL '24 hours'
  WHERE ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.environment = '$environment'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
)
SELECT
  dc.cohort AS "Cohort",
  ROUND(COUNT(DISTINCT f.deployment_id)::numeric / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT dc.deployment_id), 0) * 100, 1) AS "CFR (%)"
FROM deploy_cohorts dc
LEFT JOIN failures f ON f.deployment_id = dc.deployment_id
GROUP BY 1

panels.description: ⚠️ By deployer's seat status. Sparse incidents → large swings. No significance testing. Observational only.

panels.title: MTTR: Copilot vs Non-Copilot

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
),
failure_deploys AS (
  SELECT
    d.deployment_id,
    d.environment,
    d.created_at AS failed_at,
    CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS cohort,
    LEAD(d.created_at) OVER (PARTITION BY d.environment ORDER BY d.created_at) AS recovered_at
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  LEFT JOIN deployment_pull_requests dpr ON dpr.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  LEFT JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.number = dpr.pr_number
  LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
  WHERE ds.state = 'failure'
  AND d.environment = '$environment'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
)
SELECT
  cohort AS "Cohort",
  ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
    ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (recovered_at - failed_at)) / 3600.0
  )::numeric, 1) AS "Median MTTR (hours)"
FROM failure_deploys
WHERE recovered_at IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1

panels.description: ⚠️ Proxy MTTR (deploy gap) by deployer's seat status. Often N=1 per cohort — median of one event is not a trend.

options.content:
## 📖 Change Fail Rate: Copilot vs Non-Copilot — Learning Guide

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/deployments/deployments)
GitHub Issues (incident label) + GitHub Deployments API + Copilot seats

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`deployments`, `issues`, `copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Compares change failure rate (incident co-occurrence / deployments) between deployer cohorts.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
),
deploy_cohorts AS (
  SELECT
    d.deployment_id,
    CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS cohort
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_pull_requests dpr ON dpr.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.number = dpr.pr_number
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
  WHERE ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.environment = '$environment'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
),
failures AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT d.deployment_id
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  JOIN issues i ON i.labels::jsonb @> '[{"name":"incident"}]'
    AND i.created_at BETWEEN d.created_at AND d.created_at + INTERVAL '24 hours'
  WHERE ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.environment = '$environment'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
)
SELECT
  dc.cohort AS "Cohort",
  ROUND(COUNT(DISTINCT f.deployment_id)::numeric / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT dc.deployment_id), 0) * 100, 1) AS "CFR (%)"
FROM deploy_cohorts dc
LEFT JOIN failures f ON f.deployment_id = dc.deployment_id
GROUP BY 1
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **Deployer cohort** — uses creator_login (who triggered deployment API), not the developer who wrote the code.
- **Incident sparsity** — 1 extra incident can swing a cohort's CFR by 10–20 percentage points.
- **No statistical testing** — raw percentages with no confidence intervals.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Statistical significance of the difference.
- Causality — many confounders exist.

panels.title: 📖 Change Fail Rate: Copilot vs Non-Copilot — Learning Guide

options.content:
## 📖 MTTR: Copilot vs Non-Copilot — Learning Guide

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/deployments/deployments)
`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/deployments` + `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`deployments`, `deployment_statuses`, `copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Compares median deploy-gap MTTR (proxy) between deployer cohorts.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
),
failure_deploys AS (
  SELECT
    d.deployment_id,
    d.environment,
    d.created_at AS failed_at,
    CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS cohort,
    LEAD(d.created_at) OVER (PARTITION BY d.environment ORDER BY d.created_at) AS recovered_at
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  LEFT JOIN deployment_pull_requests dpr ON dpr.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  LEFT JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.number = dpr.pr_number
  LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
  WHERE ds.state = 'failure'
  AND d.environment = '$environment'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
)
SELECT
  cohort AS "Cohort",
  ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
    ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (recovered_at - failed_at)) / 3600.0
  )::numeric, 1) AS "Median MTTR (hours)"
FROM failure_deploys
WHERE recovered_at IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **Same proxy limitation** — deploy-gap, not actual service restoration time.
- **Very sparse data per cohort** — splitting sparse failures by two cohorts means each bar may represent only 1–2 events.
- **Inconsistent cohort dimension** — this chart uses deployer login; lead time chart uses PR author. Different populations.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Whether Copilot helps teams recover faster.
- Number of failures per cohort (denominator not shown).

panels.title: 📖 MTTR: Copilot vs Non-Copilot — Learning Guide
Copilot Seats Loaded

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Copilot Seats" FROM copilot_seats

panels.description: DATA HEALTH: row count of copilot_seats. If 0, check sync logs for a silent 403 on GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats.

panels.title: Last Successful Sync

targets.rawSql: SELECT MAX(finished_at) AS "Last Successful Sync" FROM sync_jobs WHERE status = 'success'

panels.description: Source: sync_jobs. 'success' status does NOT guarantee all resources synced — a silent 403 on any fetcher still produces status='success'.

options.content:
## 📖 Copilot Seats Loaded — Learning Guide

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-user-management)
`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
Row count of copilot_seats table — a data health indicator. If 0, the last sync did not load seat data.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Copilot Seats" FROM copilot_seats
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **Silent 403 failure** — sync server logs warning on 403 but continues; table stays at 0. 'Success' sync ≠ seats loaded.
- **TRUNCATE + INSERT every sync** — unlike PRs/deployments (incremental), copilot_seats fully replaced each sync.
- **Proxy for sync health** — use alongside 'Last Successful Sync'; check server logs if this shows 0.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Whether seat data is accurate or stale.
- Breakdown by team or repository.

## 📖 Last Successful Sync — Learning Guide

### 📡 GitHub API Source
Internal sync_jobs table (no GitHub API)

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)
`sync_jobs`

### 📐 How It's Calculated
MAX(finished_at) from sync_jobs where status='success'. Shows when the last complete sync run finished.

### 🔍 SQL Query
```sql
SELECT MAX(finished_at) AS "Last Successful Sync" FROM sync_jobs WHERE status = 'success'
```

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges
- **'Successful' sync ≠ all data loaded** — job completes with 'success' while individual fetchers (e.g., Copilot seats) fail silently. Always check records_synced per resource.
- **Timestamps in UTC** — finished_at stored in UTC; Grafana displays in browser timezone.
- **sync_jobs is append-only** — each sync adds a row; table grows indefinitely.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You
- Whether all resources were fetched (check records_synced column).
- Failure reason if last job has non-'success' status.
